Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jul-17-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONERS NORTHERN CALIFORNIA TILE INDUSTRY LABOR MANAGEMENT, NORTHERN CALIFORNIA MASONRY INDUSTRY, DAVID JACKSON, AND RONALD BENNET'S Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

In settling this writ of mandamus case, the parties agreed that plaintiffs may seek attorney fees and costs related to the first and second causes of action, which regarded the California Public Records Act. (See Govt. Code 6259(d).) However, the parties also agreed that plaintiffs are "not entitled" to attorney fees or costs related to the third, fourth and/or fifth causes of action, which regarded plaintiffs' "due process rights."

Thus, to determine "reasonable" attorney fees and costs under 6259(d), I would have to allocate fees between the two sets of causes. But I have not been provided needed information to do so. Some of the time entries provided bear the legend "time reduced for intervenor-issues work," but I am not told what that work was or how much time was reduced. Other time entries do not include the legend, leaving me to guess whether any reduction was made - and if not, why not. Also, plaintiffs' counsel declares that he has "deleted entries;" my analysis would be aided by knowing what those entries were.

Plaintiffs' "motion for an award of attorneys' fees and costs" is therefore denied without prejudice. Should plaintiffs elect to re-file their motion, the hearing shall be set on a Friday.

Plaintiff's counsel to submit a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/RBU)
